The most advanced vaccine candidate, PRV-101, had a Phase 1 follow-up study released as a preprint in February 2026. A vaccine requires successful completion of Phase 3 clinical trials and regulatory approval before it is widely considered "developed" and added to a general encyclopedia list like this one. Since there is less than a month until the final resolution date, it is virtually impossible for a vaccine to be approved and added to the Wikipedia article during this timeframe. I assign a baseline 5% probability accounting for rogue Wikipedia edits adding the Phase 1 candidate (that remain un-reverted) or other unpredictable events, slightly increasing to 6% for the later date due to standard horizon uncertainty.
